page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-12 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 vCPUs, which correspond to 6 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The system is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is provided by a bundled NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not included in this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by a tradeoff between CPU and GPU capabilities. The instance exhibits weak single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng single-core score of 1198.28 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1063.0. Conversely, the integrated NVIDIA L4 GPU delivers top-tier performance for small and medium large language model inference. Prompt processing for a 135M model reaches 11875.13 tokens per second, while text generation for a 7B model achieves 52.93 tokens per second. General multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g2-standard-12 offers a specialized resource density that is highly optimized for workloads capable of offloading computation to the GPU. Because single-threaded CPU performance is limited, this server is less efficient for CPU-bound sequential processing. It is best suited for workloads such as medium-scale LLM inference, graphics rendering, image processing, and GPU-accelerated database tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-12 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring 12 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor, 48.0 GB of system memory, and a dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. While single-core CPU benchmarks are weak, the instance delivers top-tier performance in small and medium LLM inference tasks, processing up to 11875.13 tokens per second for a 135M model. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. Lacking local storage, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for GPU-centric workloads that do not require high single-threaded CPU performance. It is best suited for LLM inference, image processing, and ray tracing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-12 is a graphics-optimized server with 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU. It delivers top-tier LLM inference performance despite weak single-core CPU scores.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-12 features 12 vCPUs, 48GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, delivering top-tier performance for medium LLM inference.
tagline: A graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated GPU acceleration for efficient small and medium large language model inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM
Features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 48.0 GB of system memory
Delivers top-tier prompt processing and text generation for small and medium LLMs
Exhibits weak single-core CPU performance but average multi-core capabilities
Suited for GPU-accelerated inference, image processing, and ray tracing
